These warm environments and plentiful prey make South Carolina ideal for snakes.

This silent evidence helps identify and locate regions, aiding in criminal investigations.

stories.title: Quote of the day by Augustus: 'We write our names in the sand and then the waves roll in and...'
stories.summary: A thought-provoking quote from Neil Gaiman's comic series suggests that names written in sand reflect life's transient beauty, although it is incorrectly credited to Emperor Augustus Caesar. This notion echoes ancient philosophical themes of mortality and the futility of permanence. In contrast to the perceived eternal nature of digital existence, recognizing impermanence can free us from the burden of seeking to leave an indelible mark.

stories.title: Quote of the day by Cyrus the Great: 'All men have their frailties; and whoever looks for a friend...'
stories.summary: Cyrus the Great, an ancient king, cautioned against the pursuit of flawless friendships. He recognized that accepting human imperfections contributed to the foundation of a strong empire. In today's world, technology magnifies our quest for perfect relationships, often resulting in loneliness. This unrealistic pursuit stifles meaningful connections. By embracing imperfections, we can nurture deeper and more resilient bonds with one another.

stories.title: This billionaire lived six months a year on his 233-foot superyacht to avoid paying land taxes

A trailblazer in digital technology and space tourism, he eventually acquired an even grander superyacht to enhance his aquatic adventures.

stories.title: Indian bonds rise on steady oil, easing US rate-hike fears
stories.summary: On Thursday, Indian government bonds experienced an upturn as oil prices stabilized alongside declining U.S. rate hike expectations, enhancing investor confidence. The market eagerly anticipates updates on India's possible inclusion in the Bloomberg index this month, a move that could significantly boost foreign investments in Indian debt markets. Bond yields have decreased for the second consecutive session, mirroring the trends of U.S. Treasury yields.

stories.title: BHEL shares jump 4% after Maharatna PSU posts net profit of Rs 377 crore in Q1, revenue jumps 40%
stories.summary: BHEL reported a consolidated Q1 FY27 net profit of ₹377 crore against a year-ago loss, driven by 40% revenue growth and a sharp recovery in operating margins. Strong power segment performance lifted sentiment, sending the PSU stock over 4% higher.

stories.title: Quote of the day by Joel Greenblatt: "When it comes to long-term investing, doing “less” is often “more”
stories.summary: Value investor Joel Greenblatt advises that less action yields more long-term investment gains. Patience and disciplined holding outperform frequent trading and market timing. Compounding wealth requires staying invested in strong businesses through market ups and downs. Avoiding emotional reactions to price swings is crucial for investment success. Resisting the urge to act and letting investments grow proves most rewarding.

stories.title: Wipro declares interim dividend worth Rs 2/share, fixes record date. Check details
stories.summary: Wipro declared an interim dividend of ₹2 per share, with July 27 fixed as the record date. The IT major reported modest Q1 FY27 profit and revenue growth, issued cautious quarterly guidance and reiterated its focus on AI-led business transformation.

stories.title: Larry Page, Sergey Brin get $15 billion richer as Warren Buffett says Alphabet bet was his call
stories.summary: Warren Buffett revealed he personally initiated Berkshire Hathaway’s investment in Alphabet, lifting the stock nearly 4% and adding over $15 billion to the combined wealth of Google co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin. Berkshire now holds Alphabet among its top five investments, while strong AI-driven growth, cloud expansion, and increased spending plans continue to support investor confidence.

stories.title: Tech Mahindra Q1 Results: Net profit rises 28% YoY to Rs 1,465 crore, revenue up 18%
stories.summary: Tech Mahindra announced a consolidated net profit of Rs 1,465 crore for the first quarter. This represents a substantial 28% increase compared to the previous year's earnings. The company also saw its net profit rise over 8% sequentially from the prior quarter. Revenue from operations experienced an 18% year-on-year jump to Rs 15,712 crore. These results reflect strong financial performance for the IT services major.

stories.title: Wipro Q1 Results: Profit flat YoY at Rs 3,352 crore; co declares Rs 2/share dividend
stories.summary: Wipro Q1 Results: Wipro reported a modest 1% year-on-year rise in June-quarter net profit to Rs 3,352 crore, while revenue grew 11%. The IT major declared an interim dividend of Rs 2 per share, but maintained a cautious outlook for the September quarter, guiding for flat to slightly negative sequential IT services revenue growth.

stories.title: Market Wrap: Sensex ends flat, Nifty holds 24,050 as Iran-US conflict keeps optimism in check
stories.summary: Sensex gained a mere 1 point to end at around 77,187, and Nifty 50 dropped around 6 points to close at nearly 24,073 on Thursday. Broader markets slipped into the red, with Nifty Midcap 100 and Nifty Smallcap 100 indices closing up to 0.4% lower.

stories.title: Rupee closes at 96.34 vs USD; firmer Asia FX, oil dip offer little comfort as NDF maturities pinch
stories.summary: On Thursday, the Indian rupee experienced a slight decline versus the dollar, influenced by dollar demand associated with expiring non-deliverable forward contracts. Meanwhile, most Asian currencies showed a strengthening trend, and the dollar index maintained stability. Additionally, Brent crude oil prices fell below eighty-five dollars per barrel, while India's Nifty 50 index remained nearly unchanged at market close.

stories.title: Warren Buffett issues new warning, says tough to find value in market when everybody prefers gambling
stories.summary: Warren Buffett has warned that value investing is becoming harder as speculative trading gains popularity. Calling today's markets increasingly driven by gambling, the Berkshire Hathaway chairman urged investors to stay patient, focus on quality businesses and avoid chasing short-term market excitement despite heightened volatility across global equities.
